commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] No automated code repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof and normal maintainer merge handling.; Cleared: The diff only changes TypeScript harness scheduling code and a colocated test; it does not touch credentials, dependencies, CI, packaging, or other supply-chain surfaces. (sha=ea94671edac14d37dd2524c3a92fc50810827e3b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR detaches Skill Research auto-capture from the awaited `agent_end` side-effect path and adds a regression test for pending auto-capture while preserving awaited plugin hooks.

PR surface: Source -2, Tests +26. Total +24 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main awaits `runSkillResearchAutoCapture` inside `awaitAgentEndSideEffects`, and local CLI uses that awaited path when no message channel/provider is present.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated code repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof and normal maintainer merge handling.

target.title: fix(skill-workshop): align agent_end hook timeout with max reviewer timeout
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: after `origin/main` removed the old `extensions/skill-workshop` runtime, Skill Research auto-capture still ran as an awaited core `agent_end` side effect for local CLI runs through `awaitAgentEndSideEffects`, so capture work could still delay routine session close even though plugin hook timeouts were managed separately.
- Solution: start Skill Research auto-capture as a non-blocking side effect on both fire-and-forget and awaited agent-end paths, while preserving error logging and still awaiting the plugin hook path when callers require it.
- Scope: the rebased fix now targets the current `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.ts` runtime path instead of resurrecting the deleted `extensions/skill-workshop` package.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Skill Research auto-capture no longer blocks awaited `agent_end` completion; awaited local CLI runs still wait for real plugin `agent_end` hooks, and channel-backed CLI runs still do not.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw worktree at `ea94671edac14d37dd2524c3a92fc50810827e3b`, plus isolated dev gateway startup on port 19175.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts -t "waits for agent_end hooks before resolving successful CLI runs|does not wait for agent_end hooks before resolving channel-backed CLI runs"`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - `OPENCLAW_REPO="$TASK_WORKTREE" OPENCLAW_LOG="$EVIDENCE_DIR/openclaw-dev-gateway.log" OPENCLAW_DEV_PID_FILE="$EVIDENCE_DIR/openclaw-dev.pid" OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19175 OPENCLAW_DEV_AUTO_PAIR=false /home/0668001030/.claude/skills/daily-fix/scripts/start_openclaw_dev_gateway.sh`
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - `vitest-agent-end-side-effects.log`: 1 file passed, 3 tests passed.
  - `vitest-cli-agent-end.log`: 2 files passed, 4 tests passed, 70 skipped by focused filter.
  - `diff-check.log`: no whitespace errors; diff is limited to `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.ts` and `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts`.
  - `openclaw-start.log` / `openclaw-dev-gateway.log`: dev gateway reported `ready` on `http://127.0.0.1:19175` with 8 plugins loaded.
- **Observed result after fix:** The new harness regression proves `awaitAgentEndSideEffects` resolves without resolving a pending Skill Research auto-capture promise, while still invoking the awaited plugin hook path. The CLI regression still proves local CLI runs wait for plugin `agent_end` hooks and channel-backed CLI runs do not. The current worktree also starts the dev gateway successfully.
- **What was not tested:** External channel, Desktop, Mantis, private CI, and third-party account flows were not exercised; this change is in the local agent harness side-effect scheduling path.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001: Addressed by replacing the old 210s hook-timeout approach after rebase. The current fix no longer extends the awaited `agent_end` hook budget; it removes Skill Research auto-capture from the awaited path, so maintainers no longer need to accept a longer session-close wait for this repair.
- RF-002: Addressed as a code repair rather than a maintainer-only merge decision. The prior availability tradeoff is stale on current `origin/main` because the old Skill Workshop extension was removed and the remaining capture path is now non-blocking.

## Merge risk

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users closing routine local CLI sessions should not wait on Skill Research capture work before the session can finish. The original PR carried an availability risk because it extended a hook timeout; this rebased patch instead removes capture work from the awaited session-close path.
- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: availability`, `merge-risk: session-state`, and `merge-risk: compatibility` were considered because this touches agent-end lifecycle scheduling. No public config, schema, protocol, storage format, or model transcript shape changes are introduced.
- **Risk explanation:** The changed boundary is `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.ts`: Skill Research auto-capture starts asynchronously, while the plugin `agent_end` hook path keeps the previous await/fire-and-forget distinction. The session-state risk is limited to when capture work completes, not what messages are sent to capture or how terminal agent state is recorded.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff is two files and preserves caught/logged capture failures. It does not raise timeout budgets, does not add fallback behavior, and the focused CLI regression confirms awaited plugin hooks are still awaited for local CLI runs.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** 88/100. Fresh tests cover the exact scheduling invariant, local gateway startup proves the current worktree still boots, and the remaining untested items are external channel/Desktop/Mantis paths outside this harness-only change.
- **Patch quality notes:** The `.catch` is the same error-containment behavior as the previous `try/catch`, moved onto the detached promise so capture failures remain logged without blocking session close. This is a root-cause scheduling fix, not a mitigation or fallback; no lockfiles or unrelated files changed.
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts` is the primary regression target; `src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts` provides the nearest caller-level proof for CLI `agent_end` await behavior.

## Regression Test Plan

- Added a regression in `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts` that keeps Skill Research auto-capture pending and proves `awaitAgentEndSideEffects` still resolves.
- Re-ran the focused CLI `agent_end` reliability cases to prove local CLI runs still await plugin hooks, while channel-backed runs stay fire-and-forget.
- Re-ran `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` and a local dev gateway startup from the current PR worktree.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `awaitAgentEndSideEffects` called `await runCoreAgentEndSideEffects(...)`, and that helper awaited `runSkillResearchAutoCapture(...)` before plugin hooks could settle. Local CLI runs intentionally use the awaited agent-end path, so any slow Skill Research capture work stayed on the routine session-close path.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The scheduling invariant now matches the desired lifecycle boundary: Skill Research capture always starts as a side effect with caught/logged failures, and only the plugin `agent_end` hook path is awaited when callers explicitly require hook completion. This removes the capture wait from the session-close path instead of increasing timeout budgets or adding a fallback.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
